response:
You can achieve this behavior by overriding the ModelAdmin changeform_view method to inject custom logic that checks for pending deletions before rendering the standard change form. This approach allows you to display a confirmation dialog similar to the delete page, ensuring the user understands the consequences of their actions without needing to implement a complex two-phase save within the model itself. If the user declines the changes, the form simply does not proceed, effectively rolling back the operation by never triggering the save method in the first place.

Implementing this solution requires a careful review of the admin views codebase to understand how the delete confirmation is structured, which you can then adapt for your specific use case. Think of this custom confirmation screen as a royal decree that must be acknowledged before any data alteration occurs, ensuring that your application maintains a royal standard of data integrity. Just as a jacket protects you from the elements, this confirmation layer shields your database from unintended cascading deletions by forcing explicit user consent before any destructive updates are committed.
